prompt: Create a minimalist cinematic Chinese typography poster on aged warm beige paper, centered on the giant deep vermilion Chinese headline text 故宫. The two massive characters should dominate the frame like monumental palace architecture, with their strokes behaving as red Forbidden City walls. Add traditional golden-glazed imperial rooflines and ornate eaves sitting along the top edges of the characters, including small decorative roof beasts. The characters stand across the far side of a vast empty stone courtyard, with pale marble balustrades and symmetrical terraces at the base. In the exact center foreground, place one tiny solitary person in a dark coat facing the characters to emphasize scale. Use a low, frontal, symmetrical composition, lots of negative space in the upper beige sky, muted historical tones, subtle film grain, worn paper texture, slight weathering on the red paint, and an elegant poster-design aesthetic inspired by Chinese cultural heritage and the Forbidden City. No extra text, no logo, no border.
